sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.44 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: Prove or disprove that for all, but finitely many, primes$p$the group$$G_p= < a, b a^2=b^p=(ab)^3=(b^rab^{-2r}a)^2=1 > ,$$-1ex where$r^2 + 1 0 \, ({ mod}\, p)$, is infinite. A solution of this problem would have interesting topological applications. It was proved with the aid of computer that$G_p$is finite for$p 17$.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
